name: Fried Eels with Creamed Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Skin and clean the eels carefully, cutting them into 3-inch pieces. Sprinkle with salt and leave for an hour.
2. Rinse and dry thoroughly. Dip in beaten egg, then roll in bread crumbs.
3. Fry in plenty of butter until golden brown. Serve with the browned butter.
4. Creamed Potatoes: Melt a tablespoon of butter, add 1 1/2 tablespoons flour and a little milk. Stir over a low flame until thickened. Season with salt and a little sugar. Cut boiled potatoes into small pieces and add to this sauce. Garnish with chopped parsley and additional butter.
When Danish anglers meet to have fried eel, they try to eat so many that the backbones form a ring around each plate.
